pattern: Because the input is already sorted, every run of a repeated value is contiguous, so a single left-to-right pass that keeps a running count per value and drops anything past the allowed cap reproduces the trimmed sequence directly.
hints: Since the array is already sorted, all copies of any one value sit right next to each other — you never need to look ahead or count a value's total occurrences in advance.
Walk the array once, remembering how many times the current value has already been kept.
Append each element only while its running count is still below the allowed limit; once a value has been kept that many times, skip every further copy of it.

edge_inputs.description: every element identical, capped down to a handful
k equal to the array length, so nothing is ever trimmed
k at its minimum, only the first copy of each value survives
a value appearing exactly the allowed number of times, none of it should be trimmed

stress.description: random sorted arrays with a variable cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
